column2: A technician needs to remove a self-contained macOS application that has no dedicated uninstaller. What is the normal removal method?
column4: Move the application's .app bundle from /Applications to the Trash.
column5: Many macOS applications are self-contained bundles. Some applications include a separate uninstall program; use that program when it is provided.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.8; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 15
column10: A+::220-1202::1.8 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::macOSFeaturesandTools Basic HighYield macOS Applications Source::Messer-v140

column2: What maintenance advantage does the macOS App Store provide for applications obtained through it?
column4: It provides a centralized place to obtain and update those applications.
column5: Applications installed from other sources may use a separate update mechanism. The App Store should not be assumed to manage every application on the Mac.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.8; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 15
column10: A+::220-1202::1.8 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::macOSFeaturesandTools Basic macOS AppStore Source::Messer-v140

column2: Which macOS system folder is the standard shared location for installed applications?
column5: This location is the usual starting point when launching, inspecting, or removing an installed application. /Library holds shared support resources, not the application collection itself.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.8; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 15
column10: A+::220-1202::1.8 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::macOSFeaturesandTools Basic HighYield macOS SystemFolders Source::Messer-v140

column2: What is the difference between /Library and ~/Library in macOS?
column4: /Library contains support resources shared across the computer, while ~/Library contains preferences and support data for the current user.
column5: The tilde means the current user's home directory, so ~/Library resolves to a path such as /Users/<username>/Library. The official objective's /Users/Library wording is treated as the user-specific Library concept, not as a literal shared path.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.8; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 15
column10: A+::220-1202::1.8 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::macOSFeaturesandTools Basic macOS SystemFolders Source::Messer-v140

column2: Why should a company use Managed Apple IDs instead of employees' personal Apple IDs for organization-assigned applications and content?
column4: Managed Apple IDs keep account and content administration under organizational control rather than tying company resources to an employee's personal account.
column5: Managed Apple IDs can be administered through Apple Business Manager and integrated with corporate identity and device-management systems. The key exam distinction is personal ownership versus managed organizational control.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.8; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 15
column10: A+::220-1202::1.8 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::macOSFeaturesandTools Basic macOS AppleID Source::Messer-v140

column2: A macOS user needs to recover an earlier version of a file from a configured backup destination. Which built-in feature should the technician use?
column5: Time Machine maintains successive backups for restoration and removes the oldest retained data as the destination fills. Disk Utility manages storage and disk images; it is not the versioned backup-and-restore feature.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.8; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, pp. 15-16
column10: A+::220-1202::1.8 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::macOSFeaturesandTools Basic HighYield macOS TimeMachine Backup Source::Messer-v140

column2: What ongoing maintenance is necessary for antimalware software on a Mac?
column4: Keep the software and its threat signatures updated.
column5: Protection becomes less effective when detection data is stale. macOS should not be treated as immune to malware simply because it has historically faced a different threat volume than Windows.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.8; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 16
column10: A+::220-1202::1.8 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::macOSFeaturesandTools Basic macOS Antivirus Source::Messer-v140

column2: A widespread macOS security issue needs an urgent fix. Which update mechanism is designed for this purpose?
column5: RSR targets urgent security issues and is managed with automatic-update settings. It complements routine operating-system and application patching; it does not replace the broader update process.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.8; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 16
column10: A+::220-1202::1.8 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::macOSFeaturesandTools Basic HighYield macOS RSR Updates Source::Messer-v140

column2: Which macOS settings area is used to arrange multiple monitors and adjust resolution, brightness, or color?
column5: The stable concept is the purpose of the Displays area. Exact interface labels and navigation paths may differ between macOS releases.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.8; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 16
column10: A+::220-1202::1.8 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::macOSFeaturesandTools Basic macOS Displays Source::Messer-v140

column2: Where in macOS settings would a technician configure a wired or wireless interface's IP and DNS values?
column5: Network settings cover interface-specific configuration, including automatic or manual addressing and more detailed options. The card tests the settings area, not a version-specific click path.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.8; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 16
column10: A+::220-1202::1.8 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::macOSFeaturesandTools Basic macOS NetworkSettings Source::Messer-v140

column2: Which macOS settings area lets a technician add or remove a printer or scanner and check its status?
column5: This area also exposes device-specific configuration and sharing options. It is the macOS counterpart to managing these peripherals through a central settings interface.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.8; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 16
column10: A+::220-1202::1.8 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::macOSFeaturesandTools Basic macOS PrintersScanners Source::Messer-v140

column2: A conferencing application cannot use the Mac's camera or microphone. Which settings category should the technician check for per-application access?
column5: Privacy controls limit access to sensitive data and hardware such as location, photos, calendars, cameras, and microphones. Grant only the access required by an approved application.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.8; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 16
column10: A+::220-1202::1.8 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::macOSFeaturesandTools Basic HighYield macOS Privacy Permissions Source::Messer-v140

column2: An approved automation utility must control keyboard and mouse input but macOS is blocking that control. Which permission category should the technician review?
column5: Accessibility permission can allow broader input or control capabilities than ordinary application access. This is distinct from Privacy permissions for sensitive data and devices such as photos, cameras, or microphones.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.8; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 16
column10: A+::220-1202::1.8 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::macOSFeaturesandTools Basic macOS Accessibility Permissions Source::Messer-v140

column2: Which macOS feature provides multiple desktops so a user can separate windows by task?
column5: Spaces are the multiple desktops themselves. Mission Control provides the overview used to see running windows and manage or move among those desktops.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.8; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 17
column10: A+::220-1202::1.8 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::macOSFeaturesandTools Basic HighYield macOS Spaces Source::Messer-v140

column2: Which macOS feature displays an overview of open windows and multiple desktops?
column5: Mission Control exposes the desktop overview. Spaces names the individual multiple desktops, so the two features are related but are not interchangeable.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.8; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 17
column10: A+::220-1202::1.8 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::macOSFeaturesandTools Basic HighYield macOS MissionControl Source::Messer-v140

column2: A technician needs to inspect securely stored passwords and certificates for a macOS user. Which built-in feature manages this information?
column5: Keychain protects credentials, certificates, secure notes, and similar secrets. FileVault has a different scope: it encrypts the Mac's disk rather than serving as a credential manager.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.8; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 17
column10: A+::220-1202::1.8 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::macOSFeaturesandTools Basic HighYield macOS Keychain Credentials Source::Messer-v140

column2: Which macOS feature searches for applications, files, images, and other content?
column5: Spotlight's primary task is search. Finder is the file manager used to browse, move, rename, or delete items after they are located.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.8; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 17
column10: A+::220-1202::1.8 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::macOSFeaturesandTools Basic HighYield macOS Spotlight Source::Messer-v140

column2: A user wants supported documents and other data to remain synchronized across their Apple devices. Which macOS-integrated service provides this function?
column5: iCloud integrates synchronization across macOS, iOS, and iPadOS. iCloud Drive is its cloud-file-storage component; Messages and FaceTime provide messaging and communication services within the same ecosystem.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.8; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 17
column10: A+::220-1202::1.8 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::macOSFeaturesandTools Basic macOS iCloud Source::Messer-v140

column2: Which macOS tool is the primary file manager for browsing, moving, renaming, and deleting files?
column5: Finder also provides access to file servers, remote storage, and related resources. Spotlight searches for content, while Finder performs the ongoing file-management work.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.8; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 17
column10: A+::220-1202::1.8 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::macOSFeaturesandTools Basic HighYield macOS Finder Source::Messer-v140

column2: What is the primary purpose of the macOS Dock?
column4: It provides quick access to applications and folders and shows which applications are running.
column5: The Dock is a launch and status area rather than a file-search tool or a multiple-desktop overview. Its placement and visibility can be customized.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.8; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 17
column10: A+::220-1202::1.8 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::macOSFeaturesandTools Basic macOS Dock Source::Messer-v140

column2: Which Apple feature family lets a user move work and supported capabilities between a Mac and other Apple devices?
column5: Continuity supports cross-device workflows such as transferring files, forwarding communication, or using capabilities from another device. It is coordinated through the Apple ecosystem and iCloud.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.8; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 17
column10: A+::220-1202::1.8 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::macOSFeaturesandTools Basic macOS Continuity Source::Messer-v140

column2: A technician needs to inspect a Mac's storage device, modify partition details, or create a disk image. Which built-in utility should be used?
column5: Disk Utility manages disks, filesystems, partitions, and disk images and can verify or repair supported filesystems. Time Machine is used for versioned backup and restoration, so the tools should not be confused.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.8; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 17
column10: A+::220-1202::1.8 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::macOSFeaturesandTools Basic HighYield macOS DiskUtility Storage Source::Messer-v140

column2: A company wants data on a lost Mac to remain unreadable without authorized startup authentication. Which macOS feature should be enabled?
column5: FileVault protects data at rest across the disk. Keychain instead stores and protects credentials, certificates, secure notes, and similar secrets within the user's environment.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.8; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 17
column10: A+::220-1202::1.8 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::macOSFeaturesandTools Basic HighYield macOS FileVault Encryption Source::Messer-v140

column2: Which macOS application provides command-line access for scripts, file management, and system configuration?
column5: Terminal exposes a command-line interface to macOS. This objective tests the tool's purpose; exact shell commands belong to the task being performed and are not duplicated as Objective 1.8 command cards.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.8; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 18
column10: A+::220-1202::1.8 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::macOSFeaturesandTools Basic macOS Terminal Source::Messer-v140

column2: A macOS application is unresponsive and needs to be stopped. Which feature should the technician use?
column5: Force Quit stops the selected application from executing and provides a list from which the application can be selected.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.8; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 18
column10: A+::220-1202::1.8 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::macOSFeaturesandTools Basic HighYield macOS ForceQuit Source::Messer-v140
